name: Gateway of India
description: An arch-monument built in the early 20th century, overlooking the Arabian Sea.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: A network of sculpted caves on an island, primarily dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya
description: The main museum in Mumbai, showcasing a vast collection of ancient to modern Indian art and artifacts.

name: Siddhivinayak Temple
description: A famous Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shri Ganesh, attracting devotees from all over the world.

name: Haji Ali Dargah
description: A historic mosque and tomb located on an islet off the coast, connected by a narrow causeway.

name: Juhu Beach
description: A popular beach famous for its lively atmosphere and a wide variety of local street food stalls.

name: Marine Drive
description: A 3.6-kilometre-long promenade, also known as the Queen's Necklace, offering stunning sunset views.

name: Colaba Causeway
description: A bustling street market known for fashion, accessories, antiques, and iconic cafes.

name: Sanjay Gandhi National Park
description: A large protected area within the city limits, offering nature trails, treks, and the ancient Kanheri Caves.

name: Wankhede Stadium
description: An iconic international cricket stadium, home to the Mumbai Indians and host of many historic matches.

name: Jehangir Art Gallery
description: Mumbai's most famous art gallery, showcasing works from established and emerging Indian artists.

name: EsselWorld Amusement Park
description: A large amusement park with a variety of rides and attractions for all ages.

name: Linking Road, Bandra
description: A famous shopping destination blending street stalls with high-end boutiques, especially for shoes and bags.

name: Mohammed Ali Road
description: A legendary food street, especially famous for its non-vegetarian delicacies and sweets during festivals.

name: Mount Mary Basilica
description: A historic Roman Catholic Basilica perched on a hillock overlooking the Arabian Sea in Bandra.

name: Bandra-Worli Sea Link
description: An eight-lane cable-stayed bridge offering spectacular views of the Mumbai coastline.

name: Upvan Lake, Thane
description: A picturesque man-made lake in Thane, known for its serene environment and cultural events.

name: Vasai Fort
description: The ruins of a large fort in the Vasai-Virar region, offering a glimpse into Portuguese history.

name: Nerul Balaji Temple, Navi Mumbai
description: A major spiritual center in Navi Mumbai, modeled after the Venkateswara Temple at Tirupati.

name: Yeoor Hills, Thane
description: A hilly forested area at the Thane side of Sanjay Gandhi National Park, popular for hiking.

name: Imagicaa Theme Park
description: A major theme park on the Mumbai-Pune Expressway with a variety of rollercoasters, themed rides, and attractions.

name: Lonavala & Khandala
description: Popular twin hill stations near Mumbai, famous for their scenic viewpoints, waterfalls, and forts. A full day trip.

name: Alibaug Beach
description: The main beach of the coastal town of Alibaug, known for its dark sand and views of Kolaba Fort.

name: Phoenix Palladium Mall
description: A premier luxury shopping mall in Lower Parel with international brands, fine dining, and a cinema.

name: R City Mall, Ghatkopar
description: One of the largest malls in Mumbai, offering a massive range of retail stores, eateries, and entertainment options.

name: Global Vipassana Pagoda
description: A massive stone dome meditation hall near Gorai, containing bone relics of Gautama Buddha.

name: Kanheri Caves
description: A complex of rock-cut monuments located deep within Sanjay Gandhi National Park, representing Buddhist art.

name: Dr. Bhau Daji Lad Museum
description: Mumbai's oldest museum, beautifully restored, showcasing the city's rich history and decorative arts.

name: Hanging Gardens of Mumbai
description: Terraced gardens on Malabar Hill, offering pleasant walks and sunset views over Marine Drive.

name: Crawford Market
description: A historic market in South Mumbai, known for its architecture and vast selection of fresh produce, pets, and wholesale goods.

name: Film City, Goregaon
description: An integrated film studio complex where many Bollywood movies are shot. Guided tours are available.

name: Aksa Beach
description: A popular but more secluded beach in Malad, known for its cleanliness and natural beauty.

name: Powai Lake
description: An artificial lake in the Powai valley, surrounded by gardens and a popular spot for evening strolls.

name: Mumba Devi Temple
description: An old Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Mumbā, from whom the city of Mumbai derives its name.

name: Bandra Fort (Castella de Aguada)
description: A historic watchtower fort at Land's End, Bandra, offering stunning views of the Bandra-Worli Sea Link.

name: Chor Bazaar
description: One of the largest flea markets in India, famous for antiques, vintage items, and second-hand goods.

name: Nehru Centre
description: A cultural and scientific center in Worli, featuring a planetarium, art gallery, and discovery of India exhibit.

name: Oberoi Mall, Goregaon
description: A popular mall on the Western Express Highway with a mix of national and international brands.

name: Carter Road Promenade
description: A seaside promenade in Bandra West, known for its jogging track and a variety of food stalls and cafes.

name: Kashid Beach, Alibaug
description: A beautiful white-sand beach with clear blue waters and casuarina groves, located south of Alibaug.

name: Murud-Janjira Fort
description: A formidable marine fort situated on an island off the coastal village of Murud, accessible by boat.

name: Tiger Point, Lonavala
description: A famous cliff-top viewpoint in Lonavala that offers panoramic views of the surrounding valley.

name: Bhushi Dam, Lonavala
description: A masonry dam on the Indrayani River, popular during the monsoon when water overflows onto the steps.

name: Lohagad Fort, Lonavala
description: A historic hill fort near Lonavala, a popular destination for trekking with commanding views.

name: Karla Caves, Lonavala
description: Ancient Buddhist rock-cut caves near Lonavala, featuring a large prayer hall and intricate sculptures.

name: Inorbit Mall, Malad
description: A large shopping mall in Malad with a wide array of stores, a food court, and entertainment facilities.

name: Kolaba Fort, Alibaug
description: An old military fort situated in the sea at a short distance from the shores of Alibaug, accessible during low tide.

name: Karnala Bird Sanctuary
description: Located near Panvel, this sanctuary is a haven for bird watchers and also features the historic Karnala Fort.

name: Zaveri Bazaar
description: The largest jewellery market in Mumbai, housing thousands of shops selling precious metals and gems.

name: Gorai Beach
description: A quieter beach in the northern suburbs, accessible by a ferry, offering a more tranquil seaside experience.
